data.title: Volatility: Natural gas futures fall following record rally on Winter Storm Fern
data.description:
It's been several action-packed sessions for natural gas prices, which were on track to log their largest weekly gain in over three decades. Front-month Nymex futures (NG1:COM) surged some 60% since Monday to reach $5.656/MMBtu, but volatility has since resumed overnight. Futures have slipped back into the $4.00 range, with many an experienced trader knowing things can continue in the wild world of commodity trading.

What happened? The coldest and snowiest weather in years is about to set in nationwide, from New Mexico and Texas to New England. New York City is even bracing for temperatures in the single digits, with subzero temperatures coming to Minneapolis, Chicago and Detroit. The Arctic blast will likely impact around 230M Americans across as many as 30 states, triggering significant demand for household heating and the potential for shortages or even outages.

"While initially attributed to fears of European supply disruptions amid recent EU-US trade tensions, the reality is more complex," OANDA Group writes in a new SA article. "U.S. output sits at decade lows [as] persistently low prices disincentivized production following the record output of 2023-2024. Simultaneously, power generation demand is surging. The need to power AI data centers and metal smelting operations is outpacing futures delivery schedules, fueling this price acceleration. Stress on the system is [also] amplified by the US's role as the world's leading LNG exporter, particularly to Europe following the closure of Russian supply routes."

On watch: Popular natural gas funds like the United States Natural Gas Fund (UNG) and the ProShares Ultra Bloomberg Natural Gas ETF (BOIL) are up 22% and 10% for the week, respectively. Generator stocks have also taken off, with both Generac (GNRC) and Pioneer Power Solutions (PPSI) advancing 9% over the same timeframe. Meanwhile, major airlines have said they may face disruptions, with Delta (DAL), United (UAL), and JetBlue (JBLU) announcing they will waive change fees on certain flights impacted by Winter Storm Fern.

data.title: Ericsson shares jump on Q4 beat, dividend, and buyback plan; CEO signals further job cuts
data.description:
Ericsson’s (ERIC) U.S.-listed shares jumped 7% on Friday after the company reported fourth-quarter results that beat expectations and issued guidance, while announcing plans to propose a SEK 3.00 per share dividend for 2025 and a SEK 15 billion share buyback program.

Additionally, the Swedish telecoms equipment maker expects to continue cutting jobs, CEO Börje Ekholm said.

"You have seen that we have reduced the headcount, for example, by 5,000 over the past year, and we expect to continue reducing headcount going forward," Ekholm said in a post-earnings call.

Earlier in January, Ericsson (ERIC) said it would cut up to 1,600 jobs in Sweden.

The company reported organic sales growth in all three segments in Q4, with 12% growth in the segment Cloud Software and Services.

"For 2026, we expect the RAN market to be flat. Mission critical and enterprise markets, where we are well positioned, are expected to grow. In this environment, we plan to increase investments in defense during 2026 while continuing to optimize our cost base to support margins and cash flow generation,” Ekholm said.

The company reported a 6% year-on-year rise in sales, with growth in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, as well as South East Asia, Oceania and India, while the Americas were broadly stable and North East Asia saw a decline.”

data.title: China approves GSK’s Trelegy Ellipta for adult asthma treatment
data.description:
• GSK (GSK) said on Friday that China’s National Medical Products Administration approved a new indication for Trelegy Ellipta to treat adults with asthma, expanding its approved use beyond chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
  • The approval means that FF/UMEC/VI is the first and only single-inhaler triple therapy approved for the maintenance treatment of both respiratory conditions in the country.
  • Approval was based on GSK’s CAPTAIN study, which showed that in patients uncontrolled on inhaled corticosteroids/long-acting beta agonist (ICS/LABA), the additional bronchodilation provided by FF/UMEC/VI demonstrated significant improvements in lung function compared with FF/VI. 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: DelphX announces private placement
data.description:
• DelphX Capital Markets (DELX:CA) (DPXCF) intends to proceed with a non-brokered private placement of up to 3M units at a subscription price of C$0.05 per unit, for gross proceeds of C$150K.

  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one common share at a price of $0.08 for a period of two years from the date of issuance.

  • The net proceeds from the offering are in connection with working capital/corporate overhead.

data.title: Trump administration revising or scrapping over $83B in clean energy loans
data.description:
The Trump administration is restructuring, revising or eliminating more than $83B in Biden-era loans and conditional commitments for clean energy projects.

"This action follows an exhaustive first-year review of the previous administration's $104B principal loan obligations, including approximately $85B rushed out the door in the final months after Election Day," the Department of Energy (DOE) announced. 

The department's Office of Energy Dominance Financing [EDF] eliminated around $9.5B in government-subsidized wind and solar projects. It replaced those projects with natural gas and nuclear uprates, where possible. 

The EDF—previously known as the Loan Programs Office—has completed or is in the process of de-obligating almost $30B of the $104B Biden-era loan obligations. It is in the process of revising another $53.6B.

The DOE said the EDF currently has more than $289B in available loan authority, which makes it the largest energy lender in the world. 

"In 2026, EDF will prioritize financing energy and manufacturing projects that meaningfully contribute to U.S. energy security, grid reliability, and lowering costs for all Americans," a statement by the office read.

data.title: Amerant Bancorp gives Q4 results
data.description:
• Amerant Bancorp press release (AMTB): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.07, may not be comparable to estimates.
  • Revenue of $112.2M (+0.8% Y/Y) beats by $5.7M.
  • Net Interest Margin (“NIM”) was 3.78%, down compared to 3.92%.
  • Average yield on loans was 6.73%, compared to 6.93%.
  • Average cost of total deposits was 2.34%, compared to 2.41%.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: INTC, ISRG, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were hovering along the flatline early Friday following back-to-back gains in major averages on easing geopolitical fears.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Ericsson (ERIC) +8% - Shares jumped after delivering stronger-than-expected Q4 results, with organic sales up 6% Y/Y and adjusted gross margin improving to 48% from 46.3% on better performance in Cloud Software and Services. Adjusted EBITA rose to SEK 12.7B from SEK 10.2B, lifting the margin to 18.3% from 14.1%, while free cash flow before M&A came in at SEK 14.9B. The board plans to propose a SEK 3.00 dividend for 2025 (up from 2.85) and a SEK 15B share buyback. Looking ahead, Ericsson expects elevated restructuring charges in 2026, amortization of around SEK 0.5B per quarter, and continued macro and tariff uncertainty. For Q1, Networks sales are expected to track roughly in line with 3-year seasonality with gross margin guidance of 49%–51%, while Cloud Software and Services sales are projected to grow below seasonal trends; Enterprise comparisons reflect prior divestitures and deconsolidated operations.
  • Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) +3% - Shares rose after reporting Q4 results that topped expectations, supported by strong growth in da Vinci system placements and procedure volumes. The installed base of da Vinci systems climbed 12% Y/Y to 11,106, while the Ion system base jumped 24% to 995. Combined worldwide procedures increased 18%, with da Vinci procedures up 17% and Ion up 44%, and quarterly placements rose to 532 units from 493 a year ago. Revenue grew 19% to $2.87B. For FY2026, the company forecasts da Vinci procedure growth of 13%–15% (vs. 18% in 2025), a non-GAAP gross margin of 67%–68%, including a ~1.2% tariff impact, and non-GAAP operating expense growth of 11%–15%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Intel (INTC) -13% - Shares plunged after posting stronger-than-expected Q4 results but issuing Q1 guidance that fell short of expectations. The company projected revenue of $11.7B–$12.7B, with the $12.2B midpoint below the $12.56B consensus, and said adjusted EPS will be roughly breakeven vs. the $0.08 analysts expected. CFO David Zinsner flagged that the ongoing server CPU shortage will leave Intel’s available supply at its lowest point this quarter before improving later in the year. While he noted that demand trends remain solid and the rapid adoption of AI continues to underscore the importance of the x86 ecosystem, Intel’s outlook also called for adjusted gross margins of 32.3%.
